title: Ticketly Project Profile
description: Per-project context Ticketly gathers before generating a backlog: an optional company, the project, the user-supplied tech stack and architecture, and the agreed ticket-ID prefix scheme. Everything here is supplied or confirmed by the user; Ticketly never invents a stack or scope.

company.description: Company / organization this project is for. OPTIONAL. When present it must be supplied by the user — Ticketly never invents it. Omit it on an existing-project run where asking for a company is awkward.

project.description: Project name this profile belongs to, supplied by the user, e.g. 'Demo Project'.

summary.description: Optional one- or two-sentence description of what the project is, in the user's words.

stack.description: The technologies the project uses, as described by the user. Every field is optional: only record what the user actually states. Never fill a layer the user did not mention.

frontend.description: e.g. ['Next.js', 'TypeScript', 'Tailwind']. Empty or absent if the project has no frontend.

infra.description: Hosting, CI/CD, containers, queues, e.g. ['Docker', 'GitHub Actions', 'Fly.io'].

other.description: Anything that does not fit the layers above, e.g. ['Stripe', 'Auth0'].

architecture.description: How the system is shaped, in the user's words. Captured from the discussion; not inferred.

notes.description: Free-form description of the architecture (monolith vs services, data flow, key decisions).

name.description: Component / module name, e.g. 'Web app', 'API service', 'Worker'.

components.description: The major moving parts of the system. May be empty if the user has not broken it down yet.

prefixes.description: The agreed ticket-ID prefix scheme. Ticketly suggests these from the stack/architecture; the user confirms or edits before any tickets are generated. Each prefix becomes an epic theme (EPIC-<PREFIX>) and its tasks (<PREFIX>-NNN).

prefix.description: 2-5 uppercase letters, e.g. 'API', 'WEB', 'DB', 'AUTH'. Matches the ticket schema's ID prefix.

domain.description: What this prefix groups, e.g. 'Backend API', 'Frontend web app', 'Database & migrations'.
